title: Runtime Scheduling SSOT
summary: C5 Runtime Scheduling owns which runtime can claim and execute a task, runtime capability eligibility, runtime lease pinning, and heartbeat meaning.
Reader documentation is generated from this manifest so scheduling boundaries are expressed as executable evidence instead of prose-only guidance.

invariants_index.title: Runtime Scheduling SSOT: Invariants
summary: Scheduling invariants are split by concept so claim eligibility and daemon implementation contracts stay independently reviewable.

source_checks.name: c5-package-boundary
source_checks.contains: Package scheduling owns the C5 Runtime Scheduling domain

source_checks.name: capability-only-gate
source_checks.contains: EvaluateCapability applies the C5 pre-submit scheduling gate

source_checks.name: pure-runtime-selection
source_checks.contains: It is pure C5 logic: no locks

source_checks.name: lease-runtime-pin
source_checks.contains: The (RuntimeID, CapabilityFingerprint) pair IS the runtime pin

source_checks.name: provider-session-boundary
source_checks.contains: task DB runtime state is sidecar state, not a task DB schema expansion

assertions: runtime scheduling dispatches by task requirements and capability snapshots, not provider binary version strings
runtime scheduling keeps provider FSM, provider session schema, and client read models outside C5
reader docs for the C5 entrypoint and core invariants are generated from this manifest
